Package org.eventb.internal.pp.core.search

Interface ResetIterator<T>

Type Parameters:
T -
All Superinterfaces:
Iterator<T>
All Known Implementing Classes:
RandomAccessList.NiceIterator
public interface ResetIterator<T> extends Iterator<T>
An iterator that allows to traverse the list while allowing the list to be modified without throwing a ConcurrentModificationException. This iterator allows to traverse the list in only one direction, and does not allow elements to be removed through it.

A call to next() never returns an element that does not currently exist in the list. A call to next() never throws a NoSuchElementException.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    hasNext()
    Returns true if the iteration has more elements.
    void
    invalidate()
    Invalidates this iterator.
    T

    Returns the next element in the iteration.
    void
    reset()
    Resets this iterator.

    Methods inherited from interface java.util.Iterator

    forEachRemaining, remove

  • Method Details

    • reset

      void reset()
      Resets this iterator.

      Puts this iterator back at the beginning of the list.

      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the iterator has been invalidated

    • invalidate

      void invalidate()
      Invalidates this iterator.

      An iterator that is not used any more will not be garbage-collected unless a call to invalidate() is issued. Once this method is called, the iterator cannot be used any more. Once this method has been called, all other method calls on this interface will throw an IllegalStateException.

    • hasNext

      boolean hasNext()
      Returns true if the iteration has more elements. Returns false if the iteration has no more elements.

      The result of this call is valid until the underlying list is modified. For instance, a call to hasNext() on an iterator that is at the end of the list will return false until an element is added again to the end of the list.

      Specified by:
      hasNext in interface Iterator<T>
      Returns:
      true if the iterator has more elements, false otherwise
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the iterator has been invalidated

    • next

      T next()
      Returns the next element in the iteration. Returns null if the iterator is at the end of the list (i.e. if hasNext() returns false).
      Specified by:
      next in interface Iterator<T>
      Returns:
      the next element in the iteration.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the iterator has been invalidated
